issues: Code imports fetch_reddit_activity from crazy.adapters.reddit_activity as required by spec adapter_contracts.
Code uses fetch_reddit_activity output but expects columns ['date', 'ticker', 'sentiment_score'], which is NOT in the adapter contract returns ['date', 'posts', 'comments'].
Spec adapter returns do NOT include 'ticker' or 'sentiment_score' columns, so code's use of these columns is inconsistent with adapter contract.
Signal logic correctly implements volatility spike as rolling 3-day std dev > 90th percentile and contrarian entry based on avg sentiment sign, matching spec intent.
Exit logic correctly exits when volatility drops below 50th percentile or after 5 trading days, matching spec intent.
Allocation logic uses 5% per position, max 20%, halves allocation if avg daily volume < 1 million, matching spec intent.
Signal names RISK_ON/RISK_OFF/HOLD are acceptable per instructions.
Overall fail due to mismatch between adapter contract output columns and code's expected columns for signal computation.
